title: High-assurance critical core preset
targetUser: Maintainers of concurrency-heavy, financial, security, or protocol-critical core logic where selected claims need formal, model, or proof evidence.

recommendedWhen: A small critical core has claims that can be modeled, checked, or proved independently of the full application.
The risk decision justifies slower verification and explicit human approval.
Formal/model outputs can be retained as PR or release evidence.
notRecommendedWhen: The change is routine UI, copy, scaffolding, or a one-off script.
No selected claim, invariant, or formalizable property has been identified.
The team would treat formal summaries as automatic approval rather than evidence for human review.

requiredInputs.name: Selected assurance claims
requiredInputs.description: Identifies which critical properties justify high-assurance evidence.

requiredInputs.name: Formal or model-checking input
requiredInputs.description: Provides the model, proof, or constraint system for selected critical claims.

requiredInputs.name: Context Pack and Boundary Map
requiredInputs.description: Limits high-assurance scope to a reviewable implementation slice and its dependencies.

requiredInputs.name: ExecPlan v2 with evidence requirements
requiredInputs.description: Captures stop rules, evidence requirements, rollback, and human approval threshold.

startingCommand.purpose: Run the broad formal/model/proof summary lane for selected critical claims.

defaultVerificationCommands.purpose: Confirm the high-assurance slice does not silently expand beyond approved boundaries.
Aggregate available formal/model/proof evidence for selected claims.
Keep the baseline PR-quality lane alongside formal evidence.
Render claim/evidence status for human approval; this command does not approve or merge.

expectedArtifacts.reviewPurpose: Shows formal/model/proof lane status and unavailable tool gaps.
Connects selected claims to formal, runtime, and human-review evidence.
Shows whether high-risk labels or assurance enforcement changed PR policy outcome.
Keeps ordinary PR checks visible even when formal evidence is emphasized.

evidenceSurfaces.description: Human maintainer approval remains required for high-risk claims; evidence does not self-approve.

escalationRule.trigger: Escalate when selected critical claims are unresolved, formal tools are unavailable, counterexamples are found, or risk remains high after mitigation.
escalationRule.action: Keep the claim unresolved or waived with explicit evidence; do not promote to merge/release approval without a human policy decision.

optionalCommands.purpose: Run a focused CSP lane for protocol/deadlock-style risks when applicable.
Run a focused TLA+ lane when a bounded state model is available.

specVerificationKit.guidance: Use the minimum profile to keep traceable tests alongside selected formal/model/proof lanes.

specKitBridge.guidance: Import Spec Kit artifacts only when they define the selected critical claims or task plan.
